name: Indiana
description: The Hoosier State — corn, basketball, the 500, and a Christmas-named town.

questions.q: What is Indiana's state nickname?
What is the capital of Indiana?
Indiana was admitted to the Union as the ___ state in 1816.
What is Indiana's state bird?

source.notes: Northern Cardinal — also the state bird of six other U.S. states.

questions.q: What is Indiana's state tree?

source.notes: Liriodendron tulipifera, also called yellow poplar.

questions.q: Which river — also Indiana's state river — runs through Terre Haute and Lafayette?

source.notes: The state song is 'On the Banks of the Wabash, Far Away.'

questions.q: Which Great Lake borders Indiana on the northwest?

source.notes: Indiana has about 45 miles of Lake Michigan shoreline.

questions.q: Which world-famous auto race is held annually in Indiana on Memorial Day weekend?

source.notes: Held at the Indianapolis Motor Speedway since 1911.

questions.q: Which Catholic university is located in South Bend, Indiana?

source.notes: Famous for its football program and the 'Fighting Irish.'

questions.q: What unusually-named Indiana town receives hundreds of thousands of letters from children every Christmas?
Which 1811 battle, fought in Indiana Territory, helped propel William Henry Harrison toward the presidency?

source.notes: Inspired the 1840 campaign slogan 'Tippecanoe and Tyler too.'

questions.q: Which Shawnee leader was killed at the Battle of the Thames in 1813?

source.notes: Tecumseh's confederacy was the major Native American resistance to U.S. expansion in the Old Northwest.

questions.q: Who was Indiana's first state governor?
What was Indiana's first capital, before it moved to Indianapolis in 1825?

source.notes: Corydon was the capital from 1816 to 1825.

questions.q: Which Indiana basketball legend is from French Lick and led the Boston Celtics to three NBA titles?

source.notes: Bird grew up in French Lick and played college ball at Indiana State.

questions.q: Indiana observes how many time zones?

source.notes: Most of the state is in Eastern Time; northwest and southwest corners are in Central.

questions.q: What 1986 sports film set in fictional Hickory, Indiana, follows a small-town high school basketball team?

source.notes: Loosely based on the 1954 Milan High School underdog state-championship win.

questions.q: Which late-night talk show host, born in Indianapolis, hosted shows on NBC and CBS for over 30 years?

source.notes: Born in Indianapolis, attended Ball State.

questions.q: Which NBA team is based in Indianapolis?

source.notes: Founded in 1967 as an ABA franchise; joined the NBA in 1976.

questions.q: Which NFL team plays its home games at Lucas Oil Stadium in Indianapolis?
